channel.title: Latest alerts from washingtonpost.com
channel.description: An unofficial feed created by Derek Willis.

item.title: Grand jury declines to indict six Democrats who released video advising military members to reject ‘illegal orders’
item.description: The decision is the latest rebuke of the Trump administration’s push to prosecute the president’s foes. Prosecutors launched an investigation into the lawmakers — all of whom served in the military or with intelligence agencies — last year after they released the video. The lawmakers have criticized the administration’s attempts to deploy the National Guard to cities run by Democratic officials and conduct strikes on alleged drug traffickers’ boats in the Caribbean Sea and Pacific Ocean.

item.title: FDA won’t review Moderna’s application for first mRNA-based flu vaccine
item.description: The decision, which shocked company officials, comes as the FDA says it will take a stricter approach to vaccine approvals. The nation’s top vaccine regulator, Vinay Prasad, told Moderna that it lacked an “adequate and well-controlled” study, the company said in a news release Tuesday.

item.title: FBI cited debunked claims to obtain warrant for Fulton County voting records, documents show
item.description: The FBI told a federal judge that it is investigating whether any “deficiencies” in Georgia officials’ handling of the 2020 presidential election were intentional.

item.title: New photos show armed person appearing to tamper with Nancy Guthrie’s front door camera, FBI says
item.description: The images mark the first time in days that investigators have shared new details in a case that has seen very few developments since Guthrie, the mother of “Today” anchor Savannah Guthrie, was reported missing by her family on Feb. 1.

item.title: Universities are sending Trump a dangerous message
item.description: Higher education is under attack. Drop the appeasement.

item.title: Know your Super Bowl commercials in today’s On the Record
item.description: Answer now to earn your Tuesday points.

item.title: Buy-it-for-life coffee makers can save money, reduce waste and brew better, our columnist writes
item.description: That new $50 drip coffee maker on your counter? Destined for the landfill after a few years of dispensing mediocre coffee. So columnist Mike Coren went in search of a better way: Buy it for life.

item.title: Ghislaine Maxwell. Trump’s ballroom. Mortgage rates. ICE hearing. Ocean current. Figure skating. Better coffee. Catch up now.
item.description: Catch up in minutes with these 7 stories.

item.title: Carolyn Hax: Proved wrong on a minor point, husband doubles down on the blame
item.description: Carolyn Hax: Proved wrong on a minor point, husband doubles down on the blame

item.title: Another ludicrous canceling of a name from the past
item.description: Shaming the dead is an asinine culture warriors’ pastime. Now it’s the great diarist Samuel Pepys’s turn.

item.title: An AI pet in today’s On the Record
item.description: Answer now to earn your Monday points.

item.title: The best, worst and weirdest Super Bowl commercials of 2026
item.description: Is it even the Super Bowl without a Ben Affleck Dunkin’ commercial?

item.title: Trump frustrations. AI adoption. Super Bowl. Epstein files. Winter Olympics. Free tuition. Weight loss. Catch up now.
item.description: Catch up in minutes with these 7 stories.

item.title: Seahawks suffocate the Patriots to win the Super Bowl
item.description: The victory is redemption for Seattle, which lost in the final seconds of Super Bowl XLIX to New England.

item.title: Hong Kong democracy champion Jimmy Lai sentenced to 20 years in prison
item.description: Lai, a leading figure in the city’s pro-democracy movement and an ardent critic of the Chinese Communist Party, was sentenced Monday after being found guilty in December of sedition and collusion with foreign forces.

item.title: A new kind of violent extremism is on the rise, rooted in 19th-century philosophy
item.description: Amid a wave of high-profile killings in the United States, investigators have been confounded regularly by the absence of a recognizable agenda.

item.title: Still need to finish this weekend’s On the Record? We’ve got you.
item.description: Play now to finish out your score for the week.

item.title: Crossword and a coffee: Solve the Sunday puzzle now
item.description: Crossword and a coffee: Solve the Sunday puzzle now

item.title: Column | My shoulder pain was relentless. I found the answer in my mouth.
item.description: I spent more than a decade getting X-rays, CT scans and other treatments for chronic shoulder pain before I found relief in the last place I would have guessed.

item.title: Lindsey Vonn’s Olympic dream ends in violent downhill crash
item.description: Broadcasts captured audio of Vonn crying, “Oh my God!” as a buoyant crowd grew essentially silent. She was 10 days removed from a crash that tore her left ACL.

item.title: The most relevant figure to Super Bowl LX is absent from it. What do we make of Colin Kaepernick now?
item.description: The Super Bowl is being played in his former home stadium, at a societal moment that echoes the issues he forced football fans to confront. So why is he out of mind?

item.title: How America became mired in the New Misery — and how it can get its confidence back
item.description: The most powerful nation in history has lost its confidence in itself, Nicholas Eberstadt writes.

item.title: How many guesses will Keyword take you today? Try to beat the average now.
item.description: How many guesses will Keyword take you today? Try to beat the average now.

item.title: How an unsolved 1971 murder led to a surprise DNA connection and reunited long-lost siblings
item.description: A detective investigating a murder stumbled on a link between a brother and sister who’d grown up in an orphanage and hadn’t seen each other for 62 years.

item.title: Stock market hits all-time high as Dow closes above 50,000
item.description: The Dow Jones Industrial Average finished at a record 50,115.67 on Friday, rising more than 1,200 points for the day.

item.title: Trump shares, then deletes video depicting Obamas as apes
item.description: Tim Scott, the sole Black Republican in the Senate, called it “the most racist thing I’ve seen out of this White House.”

item.title: Carolyn Hax chat: Spouse treats my money as ‘our money,’ but his money is his
item.description: Carolyn Hax chat: Spouse treats my money as ‘our money,’ but his money is his

item.title: Good news: We saved the bees. Bad news: We saved the wrong ones.
item.description: The campaign to save the honeybee was based on a fallacy — one that has had unintended consequences, our columnist has found.

item.title: Sonny Jurgensen, Washington quarterback and longtime announcer, dies at 91
item.description: In a Hall of Fame career, he was known as one the NFL’s best pure passers. He later spent years as a football broadcaster.

item.title: Are Americans getting richer? New data might surprise you.
item.description: Workers have proven resilient over the past decade, despite inflation and valid affordability fears, the Editorial Board writes.

item.title: 10 questions, and don't forget the bonus wager! Finish off your week with On the Record.
item.description: Think you’ve got what it takes to earn a perfect score? Take today’s longer news quiz and complete your point total for the week.

item.title: U.S.-Russia talks. Missing girl. TrumpRx site. Epstein’s network. Hopeful Democrats. Winter Olympics. Honeybees. Catch up now.
item.description: Catch up in minutes with these 7 stories.

item.title: These patients saw what comes after death. Should we believe them?
item.description: Researchers have developed a model to explain the science of near-death experiences. Others have challenged it.

item.title: No, Billie Eilish, Americans are not thieves on stolen land
item.description: Civilization depends on secure property titles, not sincere apologies.

item.title: This actress is the star of today’s On the Record
item.description: Answer now to earn your Thursday points.

item.title: He got a light sentence in a stabbing. Then he began to threaten the victim.
item.description: A severed pinkie at a crime scene sent police looking for a nine-fingered man. The case in Montgomery County, Maryland, would include threatening letters from prison and a judge’s regret over the sentence she imposed.

item.title: ICE departures. Voting map. Trump ballroom. Savannah Guthrie. Campus diversity. Winter maps. Near-death experiences. Catch up now.
item.description: Catch up in minutes with these 7 stories.

item.title: At 82, he’s as fit as a 20-year-old. His body holds clues to healthy aging.
item.description: Scientists are studying Juan López García, a world-record-holding ultramarathoner, to learn how we can stay healthy as we age.

item.title: Supreme Court clears way for California voting map that bolsters Democrats
item.description: The ruling is a major victory for the party as it seeks to offset a nationwide push by Republicans and Trump to redraw congressional maps to gain an advantage in this year’s midterm elections.

item.title: An album of the year winner in today’s On the Record
item.description: Answer now to earn your Wednesday points.

item.title: Border czar Tom Homan says 700 ICE and CBP officers are leaving Minneapolis
item.description: The withdrawal decreases the federal footprint from about 3,000 agents to 2,300 — a significant scaling back but still much higher than the 80 who were in the Minneapolis area before Operation Metro Surge began Dec. 1, according to court records. The reduction in officers is effective immediately, he said.

item.title: Shutdown reprieve. Iranian drone. Columbus statue. Renée Good. Housing crisis. Super-ager. Best in show. Catch up now.
item.description: Catch up in minutes with these 7 stories.

item.title: How 6 of America’s oldest restaurants have lived on for over a century
item.description: At a time of great change in the restaurant industry — the world, actually — Tom Sietsema wanted to learn not just how these places have survived, but also how they thrive. As different as these six restaurants are, and despite their hardships, they share common threads.

item.title: House votes to end government shutdown, buy time for ICE talks
item.description: The Department of Homeland Security will be funded through Feb. 13 to allow for bipartisan negotiations over immigration enforcement.

item.title: U.S. shoots down suspected Iranian drone that approached aircraft carrier USS Abraham Lincoln
item.description: White House spokeswoman Karoline Leavitt said the drone was “acting aggressively” when it approached the carrier, prompting commanders to order it shot down.

item.title: Pentagon warns Scouts to restore ‘core values’ or lose military support
item.description: The relationship dates back decades, but Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth has criticized the organization for allowing girls to join and changing its name from Boy Scouts.

item.title: Carolyn Hax: Drop ‘snarky’ colleague from trip, or will the fallout be worse?
item.description: Carolyn Hax: Drop ‘snarky’ colleague from trip, or will the fallout be worse?

item.title: How millions of everyday investors are getting a tax break
item.description: A watershed change by the SEC offers American savers a way to dodge the mutual fund surprise.

item.title: A new location in today’s On the Record
item.description: Answer now to earn your Tuesday points.

item.title: Disney announces Josh D’Amaro to succeed Bob Iger as CEO
item.description: Iger returned in 2022 for a second stint as chief executive. D'Amaro currently leads Disney’s parks and resorts division.
